observations.recommendation:
We recommended and
Management agreed to:
a. Require the Municipal
Planning and Development
Council (MPDC), in
coordination with the MEO, to closely
supervise, and
evaluate the
implementation of
priority development
PPAs embodied in the
LDP and AIP; and
b. Direct Budget Officer and
Municipal Planning
Development Officer to
reassess unimplemented
projects and those with
unused balances to
address other priority
areas or developmental
projects that are needed
by the constituents.

1.6 We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to re-calculate the amount of depreciation expense in order to
adjust the carrying amount of Road Networks and account for the change
in accounting estimate in accordance with IPSAS 3 - Accounting Policies,
Changes in Accounting Estimates and Errors.

2.9 We recommended and the Management agreed:
a. Cause the preparation of the Inventory and Inspection Report for
Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) by the Property Officer/Unit to
facilitate the disposal of unserviceable properties; and
b. Direct the Disposal Committee to expedite the disposal of unserviceable
properties following the procedures set forth in Section 2.1 and 2.2 of
COA and DBM Joint Circular, to prevent further deterioration and to
realize income therefrom if found to be saleable.

3.8 We recommended and the Management agreed to require:
a. The Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer
and reconcile records of completed projects which are still included in
the CIP;
b. The Municipal Accountant to secure from the Municipal Engineer
Certificate of Completion and Certificate of Turnover and Acceptance
on the validated completed projects to be used as basis in the
reclassification of the CIP accounts to the proper PPE account;
c. The Municipal Accountant to take up the corresponding depreciation
expenses of their classified completed projects from the time of
completion until December 31, 2024, prepare adjusting entry as
necessary, and henceforth ensure reclassification of all completed
projects in the Construction in Progress account to the appropriate
asset accounts;
d. The Municipal Accountant to effect the following adjusting entry to
close the completed Public Infrastructures to the Government Equity
and transfer the same to the Registry of Public Infrastructures; and
e. Observe and follow strictly the prescribed accounting entries provided
under the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volumes I and III in transferring
the completed Public Infrastructure to the Registry of Infrastructures
at the end of the year to reflect the correct account balances in the
year-end financial statements.

4.10 We recommended and the Management agreed:
a. Require the Municipal Planning and Development Council (MPDC), in
coordination with the MEO, to closely monitor and evaluate the
implementation of the identified and prioritized development PPAs
embodied in the LDP and AIP; and
b. Direct Budget Officer and Municipal Planning Development Officer to
reassess unimplemented projects and projects with unused balance in
the 20% Development Fund to address other developmental projects
that are needed by the constituents.

5.8 We recommend that Management adopt the payroll-through-bank system
provided by the AGDB to streamline salary disbursements, minimize risks,
and promote transparency, accountability, and efficiency in financial
transactions.

6.14 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Ensure the timely utilization of the LDRRMF in accordance with the
approved LDRRMP and to utilize STF in accordance with Item 6.4
and 6.5 of the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1;
b. Direct the LDRRMC to identify and prioritize PPAs that are feasible,
doable and can be completed within the target date of
implementation taking into consideration the thrust set by the LGU
leadership;
c. Direct all concerned officials to optimize the utilization of LDRRMF
focusing on programs, projects and activities that will strengthen its
capacity to prevent and mitigate disaster risks;
d. Facilitate proper monitoring and evaluation of the utilization of fund
and assess its implementation to immediately address any arising
concerns/problems that can be mitigated.

7.10 We recommended and the management agreed to:
a. Prepare and submit a revised AIP as approved by the local
sanggunian and LCE to ensure that the release and utilization of the
unexpended balances of the LDRRMF is in accordance with
Paragraph 6.5 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-01;
b. Direct the MDRRMO through the MDRRMC to convene and decide
on the priorities and use of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF
which shall be incorporated in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction
Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP);
c. Ensure that the LDRRMFIP/Revised LDRRMFIP include under a
separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged to the
unexpended LDRRMF of previous years; and
d. Henceforth, strict compliance to NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No.
2013-01, COA Circular No. 2012-002 and other existing regulations in
the release and utilization of unexpended LDRRMF is enjoined.

8.10 We recommended and the management agreed that the:
a. The Sangguniang Bayan enact the necessary resolutions formally
authorizing the operation of each Local Economic Enterprise (LEE) to
establish a clear legal foundation and align with the requirements of
RA 7160 and the LEE Manual;
b. The Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant ensure
that separate Annual Operating Budgets (AOBs) are prepared and
maintained for each LEE, consistent with Item 5.1(f) of the LEE
Manual, and that these are properly integrated into the approved
appropriation ordinance; and
c. The Municipal Economic Enterprise and Development Office
(MEEDO), in coordination with the Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator (MPDC), prepare a comprehensive 5-Year
Business Plan for each existing LEE, covering management,
operations, staffing, risk, marketing, and financial projections, to
promote operational sustainability and compliance with Item 5.3 of
the LEE Manual.

9.6 We recommend and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to conduct a thorough analysis and reconciliation of the Due
from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts. Any discrepancies
identified should be promptly adjusted through appropriate correcting
entries to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the financial statements.
Furthermore, a copy of the reconciliation and the corresponding journal
entries should be submitted to the Commission on Audit (COA) for
verification and monitoring purposes

10.9 We recommended that the Management direct the concerned offices to
implement immediately all ongoing, delayed and unimplemented projects
and closely monitor project status to attain the program’s objectives.
Otherwise, when the project cannot be implemented for any valid reason,
the amount received from the LGSF-SBDP shall be immediately reverted
to the National Treasury, as provided in Section 4.9 of DBM Local Budget
Circular No. 151.

We recommended and
Management agreed to:
a. Ensure that all PPEs
have been provided with
a depreciation
expense and that all
data necessary have
been appropriately
recorded in lapsing
schedule; and
b. Prepare appropriate
journal entries to
correct the
depreciation and
effect necessary
adjustments to
rectify the errors and
present the asset
accounts to their
proper carrying
amount.

We recommended and
Management agreed to
direct the OIC-
Municipal Accountant
to re-calculate the
amount of depreciation
expense to adjust the
carrying amount of
Road Networks and
account for the change
in accounting estimate
in accordance with
IPSAS 3 - Accounting
Policies, Changes in
Accounting Estimates
and Errors.

We recommended and
Management agreed to
require the Municipal
Accountant to furnish
the Audit Team of
Original Journal Entry
Voucher. Further,
analyze and reconcile
the reciprocal accounts
and make necessary
adjusting entry to
reflect the correct
balances of said
accounts in the
financial statements
and furnish the Audit
Team of JEV on
corrections made
therein.

We recommended and
Management agreed to
direct the Municipal
Accountant to complete
required documents to
support the payment of
wages and claimed of
Program of Work
claimed of
Accomplishment on the
projects.

We recommended and
Management agreed to the
following:
1. Direct the Municipal
Accountant to ensure that
disbursement vouchers are
adequately supported with the
required documents such as Payrolls,
DTRs, Accomplishment
Reports and
certifications, to
confirm that the paid
salaries, wages and
allowances were valid,
actually rendered in
accordance with their
assigned tasks and their
services were necessary;
2. Require the concerned job
orders and contract of service
workers to submit their DTRs and
individual accomplishment
reports (for JOs) duly verified and
signed by their immediate
supervisors in adherence to Section
4 (6) of PD No. 1445 and Section
1.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-
001; and
3. Require the concerned personnel
to submit the payrolls, DTRs and
certification by the head of the
health facility to support the grant
of HEA to the qualified employees
and job orders.

We recommended that
Management:
a. Require the Municipal
Accountant to ensure the
submission of complete
attachment of all supporting
documents needed for all money
claims and other transactions
pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-
001;
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to
make sure that all travel claims are
dully supported by an approved
Office Order or Travel Order,
approved Itinerary of Travel,
Certificate of Appearance and
Certificate of Travel Completed;
c. Exercise prudence and diligence in
the disbursements of funds and
enforce compliance with the
provisions of DBP Budget Circular
No. 2007-3 on the payment of
honoraria of members of the BAC,
TWG, and BAC Secretariat to
prevent excessive and irregular
payments;
d. Require the HRMO/personnel in-
charge of payroll preparation to
provide the computation on the
payments of honoraria and submit
the required supporting documents
to the Municipal Accountant in
accordance with COA Circular No.
2012-001 to validate all the
payments made; and
e. Direct the Municipal Accountant to
ensure compliance with pertinent
laws and regulations on proper
documentation of disbursements
prior to signing for payment.

Stop transferring of the barangay funds in order for them to implement their own  developmental  programs  and  projects  as  envisioned in R.A. No. 7160.

Submit Utilization Report on the fund transferred to the Municipality by the barangay including documentary requirements to support the utilization of the said fund by source; and

Strictly comply with Section 5 of the DILG/DBM JMC No. 2011-1 dated       April 13, 2011 and 2020-01 dated March 27, 2020 as well as  Section 2 (a) of R.A. No. 7160 in granting local autonomy to local government units specifically the barangay government.
observations.reason:
The Barangays continue transferring of Brgy. Funds to the Municipality.


Not Implemented.

The Municipality did not submit Utilization Report on the Fund transferred to them.

Not Implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed to:
Create an Inventory Committee and come up with a Physical Inventory Plan to facilitate the submission of  the RPCPPE to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year;
Cause the reconciliation of the property records with the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments, if any; and
Require the Municipal Accountant and the Supply Officer to immediately maintain PPELC and PC, respectively, per category of property, plant and equipment, pursuant to Sections 119 and 120 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
Exert effort in the processing of Certificate of Title of the untitled lots in order to establish absolute ownership and protect the legal interest of the LGU pursuant to Section 148 of COA Circular 92-386; and
Allocate fund intended for the survey/relocation and titling of these lots. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate and supervise the proper proceedings for the land titling and to cause the immediate transfer to the Municipality to ascertain the ownership.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
Stop receiving fund transfer from barangay funds in order for them to implement their own developmental programs and projects.
Submit Utilization/Accomplishment Report on the fund transferred to the municipality by the barangay including documentary requirements to support the utilization of the said fund.
Strictly comply with Section 5 of the DILG/DBM JMC No. 2011-1 dated       April 13, 2011 and 2020-01 dated March 27, 2020 as well as  Section 2 (a) of RA 7160 in granting local autonomy to local government units specifically the barangay government.

We recommended and the management agreed for  the immediate submission of the Reports of Check Issued and Disbursement Vouchers and their  supporting  documents, Reports  of   Cash Disbursements and Payrolls also, see to  it  that  the  provisions  of   Sections  100   and  122  of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately comply and submit the documentary requirements to enable the Audit Team to evaluate the transactions amounting to ₱19,779,317.87.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to throughly verify each disbursement as to the completeness and propriety of the supporting documents to strengthen internal control in the disbursement process; and
Adhere to the procedures and requirements provided in Section 4 (6) of          P.D. 1445, Item 9.2.5 of COA  Circular No. 2012-001  dated  June 14, 2012 .

Require immediate liquidation of cash advances as soon as the purpose for which it was given has  been served and refrain from granting additional cash advances unless the previous cash advance has been settled/liquidated in strict compliance with  Section  89 P.D. 1445  and  Section  5.1.3  of  COA  Circular No. 97-9002 dated February 10, 1997;

Require an official/employee who intends to be separated from the service due to reassignment/transfer/retirement to refund unliquidated cash advances before issuance of clearance from accountabilities.

Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements and submit promptly the same to the Office of the Auditor regularly within the period prescribed under Section 74 of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.reason: Delayed preparation was due to voluminous work assigned to the personnel who prepared the bank reconciliation.

observations.recommendation:
Immediate submission of the Reports of Check Issued and Disbursement Vouchers and their supporting documents, Reports of Cash Disbursements and Payrolls, as well as the Utilization Report for CY 2020 specifically Covid 19 Pandemic related transactions; and
Henceforth, strictly comply with pertinent provisions of Section 4(6), 100 and 122 of P.D No. 1445.

Require the Inventory Committee to submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAs) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I. Likewise, assistance by other offices be extended to Supply Officer to audit inventory.

Cause the reconciliation of the property records with the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments, if any.


Require the Municipal Accountant and the Property Officer to immediately maintain PPELC and PC, respectively, per category of property, plant and equipment, pursuant to Sections 119 and 120 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.reason: For reconciliation of the property records with the accounting records is still on-going.

observations.recommendation:
Maximize utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation and regular monitoring of GAD related programs, and that projects and activities that addressed gender issues and women empowerment and gender equality should be prioritized  in order to attain the objective for which the funds are provided in conformity with Section1.2 of Joint Circular No. 2004-1 of DBM, NEDA and NCRFW and Section 4.1 (c.1.4) of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA;

Refrain from realignment of funds to other purposes and utilize  only the planned programs and projects that are related to the appropriated fund  pursuant  Section 4(1) of P.D. No. 1445 ;
observations.reason: There is still programs and projects that were not fully implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
Require immediate liquidation of cash advances as soon as the purpose for which it was given has  been served and refrain from granting additional cash advances unless the previous cash advance has been settled/liquidated in strict compliance  with  Section  89 of P.D. No. 1445  and  Section  5.1.3  of COA Circular  No. 97-9002 dated February 10, 1997; and
Require an official/employee who intends to be separated from the service or due to reassignment/transfer/retirement to refund unliquidated cash advances before issuance of clearance from accountabilities.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to prepare monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements and submit promptly the same to the Office of the Auditor regularly within the period prescribed under Section 74 of P.D. No. 1445.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
Stop transferring of the barangay funds in order for them to implement their own  developmental  programs  and  projects  as  envisioned in R.A. No. 7160.
Submit Utilization Report on the fund transferred to the Municipality by the barangay including documentary requirements to support the utilization of the said fund by source; and
Strictly comply with Section 5 of the DILG/DBM JMC No. 2011-1 dated       April 13, 2011 and 2020-01 dated March 27, 2020 as well as  Section 2 (a) of R.A. No. 7160 in granting local autonomy to local government units specifically the barangay government.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
Immediate submission of the Reports of Check Issued and Disbursement Vouchers and their supporting documents, Reports of Cash Disbursements and Payrolls, as well as the Utilization Report for CY 2020 specifically Covid 19 Pandemic related transactions; and
Henceforth, strictly comply with pertinent provisions of Section 4(6), 100 and 122 of P.D No. 1445.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
Require the Inventory Committee to submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAs) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I. Likewise, assistance by other offices be extended to Supply Officer to audit inventory.
Cause the reconciliation of the property records with the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments, if any.
Require the Municipal Accountant and the Property Officer to immediately maintain PPELC and PC, respectively, per category of property, plant and equipment, pursuant to Sections 119 and 120 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the aforementioned supporting documents to enable the Audit Team to evaluate the BGCM, Trust Fund and LDRRMF transactions amounting to ₱14.791 million;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to throughly verify each disbursement as to the completeness and propriety of the supporting documents to strengthen internal control in the disbursement process; and
Adhere to the procedures and requirements provided in Section 4 (6) of P.D. 1445, Items 9.2 and 9.2.5 of COA  Circular No. 2012-001  dated  June 14, 2012  and  GPPB  Circular  No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020 particularly in the conduct of emergency procurement under the Bayanihan Act.

Require immediate liquidation of cash advances as soon as the purpose for which it was given has  been served and refrain from granting additional cash advances unless the previous cash advance has been settled/liquidated in strict compliance with  Section  89 P.D. 1445  and  Section  5.1.3  of  COA  Circular No. 97-9002 dated February 10, 1997;

Require an official/employee who intends to be separated from the service due to reassignment/transfer/retirement to refund unliquidated cash advances before issuance of clearance from accountabilities.

Maximize utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation and regular monitoring of GAD related programs, and that projects and activities that addressed gender issues and women empowerment and gender equality should be prioritized  in order to attain the objective for which the funds are provided in conformity with Section1.2 of Joint Circular No. 2004-1 of DBM, NEDA and NCRFW and Section 4.1 (c.1.4) of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA;

Refrain from realignment of funds to other purposes and utilize   only the planned programs and projects that are related to the appropriated fund  pursuant  Section 4(1) of P.D. No. 1445 ;

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the immediate submission of the Reports of Check Issued and Disbursement Vouchers and their supporting documents, Reports of Cash Disbursements and Payrolls, Report of Collections and Deposits and Official Receipts, Monthly Trial Balances and Financial Statements. Also, see to it that the provisions of Sections 100 and 122 of P.D. No. 1445 and  COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 are strictly complied with in a timely manner.


We recommended further the hiring of additional employees to solve the lack of manpower complement of the Accounting Office so as to avoid delayed submission of accounts.

We  recommended that management  require the  Municipal Accountant to see to it that  the monthly net take home pay of an employee should not be lower than 5,000.00 before issuing certification to lending institutions in strict compliance to Section 52 of  Republic  Act. No. 10924 also known as General Appropriation Act of  2019.
observations.reason: There is still employees with net take home pay that is lower than ₱5,000.00.

observations.recommendation:
Utilize the SEF and fully implement the  programs and projects identified for such purpose or provided under Sections 100(c) and 272 of RA 7160 in order to maximize the  benefits that students and teachers could avail under this fund; and

Stop using funds appropriated/intended for the planned programs and projects in the  20% DF  to various programs and projects related to SEF pursuant to Section 4(1) of P.D. No. 1445  otherwise  they  will  be disallowed in audit.

a. Return the unexpended/unutilized amount of the fund transferred by the barangays to the respective barangays in order for them to implement their own developmental programs and projects.
b. Strictly comply with Section 5 of the DILG/DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 and Section 2(a) of RA 7160 in granting local autonomy to local government units specifically the barangay government.

a. Minimize/lessen catering activities that are more exigent to the government service so that the municipality could have the opportunity to finance priority projects which are more useful and beneficial to the constituents.
b. Submit lacking supporting documents and comply strictly with Section 4(6) of P.D. 1445, otherwise, they will be suspended or disallowed in audit.

We recommended the immediate submission of the Reports of Check Issued and Disbursement Vouchers and their supporting documents, Reports of Cash Disbursements and Payrolls, Report of Collections and Deposits and Official Receipts, Monthly Trial Balances and Financial Statements. Also, see to it that the provisions of Sections 100 and 122 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 are strictly complied with. We recommended further the hiring of additional employees to solve the lack of manpower complement of the Accounting Office so as to avoid delayed submission of accounts.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that the monthly net take home pay of an employee should not be lower than 5,000.00 before issuing certification to lending institutions in strict compliance to Section 48 of Republic Act. No. 10924.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended to Management the following:
a. Require immediate liquidation of cash advances as soon as the purpose for
   which it was given has been served and refrain from granting additional cash
   advances unless the previous cash advance has been settled/liquidated in strict
   compliance with Section 89 P.D. 1445 and Section 5.1.3 of COA Circular
   No. 97-9002 dated February 10, 1997;
b. Require an official/employee who intends to be separated from the service due to
   reassignment/transfer/retirement to refund unliquidated cash advances before
   issuance of clearance from accountabilities.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management require the Municipal
Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Special Disbursing Officer to reconcile their
cashbook balances and subsidiary ledger balances for Cash in Bank (01-02-
010/020) and Advances for Payroll at least quarterly to correct detected errors and
adjust reconciling items in their respective books/records.

We recommended further the hiring of additional employees to solve the lack of
manpower complement of the Accounting Office and for timely reconciliation of
accounts between the Municipal Treasurer, Accountant and Special Disbursing
Officer.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following:
a. Cause the reconciliation of the property records with the accounting records and
effect necessary adjustments, if any.
b. Require the Municipal Accountant and the Supply Officer to immediately maintain
PPELC and PC, respectively, per category of property, plant and equipment,
pursuant to Sections 119 and 120 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:
a. Demand the Municipal Officials concerned to submit the Deed of Absolute Sale
   from the Landowners;
b. Prioritize the titling of the subject lots in the name of the Municipality to establish
   ownership and full control thereof;
c. After completion of the TCT, submit copy of TCT and other documentary
   requirement/s to the Audit Team to avoid audit suspension or disallowance of the
   said transactions; and
d. Henceforth, comply strictly with the Sections 4(6) and 39(2) of P.D.
   No. 1445 and Section 449 of GAAM, Volume I and Section 13.1 of COA
   Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended to Management the following:
a. Return the unexpended/unutilized amount of the fund transferred by the
   barangays to the respective barangays in order for them to implement their own
   developmental programs and projects.
b. Strictly comply with Section 5 of the DILG/DBM Joint Memorandum Circular
   No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 and Section 2(a) of RA 7160 in granting local
   autonomy to local government units specifically the barangay government.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Management:
a. Ensure prompt and full implementation of the 20% development projects and
   programs during the current year;
b. Proper planning of programs and projects should be made to refrain from
   reprogramming of funds to other purposes and utilize only the planned programs
   and projects that are related to the current year’s 20% Development Fund
   programs and projects in order to achieve the intended purposes for which the
   fund was appropriated;
c. Stop using funds appropriated/intended for the planned programs and projects in
   the 20% DF pursuant to Section 4(1) of P.D. No. 1445 otherwise they will be
   disallowed in audit; and
d. Strict compliance of Section 287 of R.A. No. 7160 and Section 5 of DILG/DBM
   and JMC No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 and Sections 3.0 – 4.0 of the DILG/
   DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management:
a. Maximize utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation and regular
   monitoring of GAD related programs, and that projects and activities that
   addressed gender issues and women empowerment and gender equality should
   be prioritized in order to attain the objective for which the funds are provided in
   conformity with Section1.2 of Joint Circular No. 2004-1 of DBM, NEDA and
   NCRFW and Section 4.1 (c.1.4) of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of
   PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA;
b. Refrain from realignment of funds to other purposes and utilize only the
   planned programs and projects that are related to the appropriated fund
   pursuant Section 4(1) of P.D. No. 1445 ;

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the immediate submission of the Reports
of Check Issued and Disbursement Vouchers and their supporting documents,
Reports of Cash Disbursements and Payrolls, Report of Collections and Deposits
and Official Receipts, Monthly Trial Balances and Financial Statements. Also, see to
it that the provisions of Sections 100 and 122 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular
No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 are strictly complied with in a timely manner.

We recommended further the hiring of additional employees to solve the lack of
manpower complement of the Accounting Office so as to avoid delayed submission
of accounts.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that management require the Municipal
Accountant to see to it that the monthly net take home pay of an employee should
not be lower than 5,000.00 before issuing certification to lending institutions in strict
compliance to Section 52 of Republic Act. No. 10924 also known as General
Appropriation Act of 2019.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Management:
1. Utilize the SEF and fully implement the programs and projects identified for
   such purpose or provided under Sections 100(c) and 272 of RA 7160 in order to
   maximize the benefits that students and teachers could avail under this fund;
and
2. Stop using funds appropriated/intended for the planned programs and projects
   in the 20% DF to various programs and projects related to SEF pursuant to
   Section 4(1) of P.D. No. 1445 otherwise they will be disallowed in audit.

a. Require the Inventory Committee to submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I. Likewise, assistance by other offices be extended to Supply Officer to audit inventory.
b. Cause the reconciliation of the property records with the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments, if any.
c. Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to maintain and reconcile the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) and Property Cards (PC), respectively, per category of property, plant and equipment, pursuant to Sections 119 and 120 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

a. Provide adequate and safe shelter for its motor vehicles and equipment to safeguard these assets from various harmful weather conditions/natural calamities and/or man-made disaster which could prolong its useful life and safe from possible loss, and to expedite acquisition of space/area; and
b. Strictly adhere to Sections 2 and 102 (2) of P.D. 1445.

a. Return the amount of the fund to the 30% QRF and require the Municipal Accountant and the OIC-MDRRMO to reclassify programs, projects and activities of LDRRMF properly;
b. Strictly comply with Sections 5.3 and 6.3 of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 and Section 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 to ensure that programs and projects are included in the LDRRMFIP and crisis and disaster which may occur in the future.

We recommended that Management strictly implement and comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 9003 on the segregation of solid wastes at source and establishment of a sanitary landfill in order to protect the environment and public health.

We recommended that Management maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation and regular monitoring of GAD related programs, projects and activities in order to attain the objective for which funds are provided and see to it that PPAs are in conformity with Section1.2 of Joint Circular No. 2004-1 of DBM, NEDA and NCRFW and Section 4.1 (c.1.4) of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA.

We recommended the immediate submission of copy of any Contracts irrespective of amount, and the corresponding supporting documents to the Auditor’s Office, within five (5) working days from perfection/issuance thereof, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, otherwise the transactions covered by the unsubmitted documents will be suspended in audit and any unjustified failure of the officials and employees concerned to comply with the requirements shall be subject to the administrative disciplinary action provided in Section 127 (a) of P.D. No. 1445. Henceforth, management should properly review transactions/contracts as to the completeness of supporting documents.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their cashbook balances and subsidiary ledger balances for Cash Local Treasury (01-01-010), Cash in Bank (01-02-010/020) and Advances for Payroll at least quarterly to correct detected errors and adjust reconciling items in their respective books/records.

Prioritize the titling of the subject  lots in the name of the Municipality to establish ownership and full control thereof;

After completion of the TCT,  submit copy of TCT and other documentary requirement/s to the Audit Team to 
Avoid audit suspension or disallowance of the said transactions; and

Henceforth, comply strictly with the Sections 4(6) and 58 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 449 of GAAM, Volume I.

Return the unexpended/unutilized amount of the fund transferred by the barangays to the respective barangays in order for them to implement their own developmental programs and projects, if any;

Strictly comply with Section 5 of the DILG/DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 and Section 2(a) of RA 7160 in granting local autonomy to local government units specifically the barangay government.

Replenish  the amount of the fund to the 30% QRF and require the Municipal Accountant and  the OIC, MDRRMO to reclassify programs, projects and activities of LDRRMF properly;

Strictly comply with Sections 5.3 and 6.3 of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 and Section 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended to Management the following:
Require immediate liquidation of cash advances as soon as the purpose for which it was given has  been served and refrain from granting additional cash advances unless the previous cash advance has been settled/liquidated in strict compliance with  Section  89 P.D. 1445  and  Section  5.1.3  of  COA  Circular No. 97-9002 dated February 10, 1997;
Require an official/employee who intends to be separated from the service due to reassignment/transfer/retirement to refund unliquidated cash advances before issuance of clearance from accountabilities.
Request for write-off of Cash Advances which can no longer be collected after complying  with  requirements   of   COA   Circular   2016 - 005   dated December 19, 2016.

In view of the foregoing, we recommend to Management the following:
Require the Inventory Committee to submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAs) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I. Likewise, assistance by other offices be extended to Supply Officer to audit inventory.
Cause the reconciliation of the property records with the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments, if any.
Require the Municipal Accountant and the Property Officer to immediately maintain PPELC and PC, respectively, per category of property, plant and equipment, pursuant to Sections 119 and 120 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended to Management the following:
provide adequate and safe shelter for its motor vehicles and equipment to safeguard these assets from various harmful weather conditions/natural calamities and/or man-made disaster to prolong its useful life and safe from possible loss,  and to expedite acquisition of space/area; and
Strictly adhere to  Sections 2  and 102 (2) of P.D. 1445.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended to Management the following:
Require the submission of lacking documents/justification, otherwise, stop and refund the payment of honoraria of several Elective Officials and Municipal Department Heads who are MCT members.
Hire MCT from non-government officials within the Municipality as required under  Section 2.2.2 of the Community Based Infrastructure Manual, Volume I  (Revised) of June 2016 of the DSWD;
Henceforth, strictly comply  with  the  provisions  stated  in Section 9 of COA Circular No.  94-013  dated   December  13, 1994 ,  DBM   Budget   Circular  No. 2007-2 dated October 1, 2007, Section 5.7.5 of COA Circular No, 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and Section 2.2.2 of the Community Based Infrastructure Manual, Volume I  (Revised) of June 2016 of the DSWD.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended to Management the following:
Return the unexpended/unutilized amount of the fund transferred by the barangays to the respective barangays in order for them to implement their own developmental programs and projects.
Strictly comply with Section 5  of the DILG/DBM  Joint  Memorandum  Circular  No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 and Section 2(a) of RA 7160 in granting local autonomy to local government units specifically the barangay government.

We recommended that Management strictly implement and comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 9003 on the segregation of solid wastes at source and establishment of a sanitary landfill in order to protect the environment and public health.

No. 1445.  Henceforth, management should properly review transactions/contracts as to the completeness of supporting documents.

We recommended that Management should:
Minimize/lessen catering services to activities that are more exigent to the government service so that the municipality could have the opportunity to finance priority projects which  are more useful and beneficial to  the constituents .
b.  Submit lacking supporting documents and comply strictly with Section 4(6) of     P.D. 1445, otherwise, they will suspended or disallowed in audit.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the immediate submission of the Reports of Check Issued and Disbursement Vouchers and their supporting documents, Reports of Cash Disbursements and Payrolls, Report of Collections and Deposits and Official Receipts, Monthly Trial Balances and Financial Statements. Likewise, require the Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately the required reports after payments as well as strict monitoring/supervision of the personnel concerned in the preparation and timely submission of required reports. Also, see to it that the provisions of Sections 100 and 122 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 95-006 dated  May 18, 1995 are strictly complied with.

In view of the foregoing, we  recommend that management  require the  Municipal Accountant to see to it that  the monthly net take home pay of an employee should not be lower than 5,000.00 before issuing certification to lending institutions in strict compliance to Section 48 of  Republic  Act. No. 10924 also known as General Appropriation Act  of  2018.

We recommended to the Management to require the Municipal Accountant to indicate the purpose of transfer of fund/s and secure authority from the concerned officials when needed in strict compliance with    Section 4(3) of P.D. No. 1445.

No. 7160 and Section 5 of DILG/DBM and Joint Memorandum Circular  No. 2011-1  dated April 13, 2011.

Circular No. 23-007 dated March 20, 2007 to  assure  reasonable cost most advantageous to the government.
